description:
You are the on-call Site-Reliability Engineer.  
A colleague has left you two artefacts in your home directory:

1. /home/user/data/ping_results.json  
2. /home/user/schemas/ping_result.schema.json  

The JSON array in ping_results.json is a chronological list of synthetic ICMP checks.  
Each element **must** respect this schema:

{
  "host"        : string,                 # e.g. "web-1"
  "timestamp"   : ISO-8601 string,        # e.g. "2023-08-01T10:00:00Z"
  "status"      : string, **"up"|"down"**
  "latency_ms"  : number|null             # RTT in milliseconds or null on failure
}

additionalProperties are **not** allowed and all four keys are required.

Your tasks:

A. Schema validation  
   • Using nothing but jq, verify that every object in /home/user/data/ping_results.json  
     1) contains all four keys above, and  
     2) has .status equal to either "up" or "down".  
   • Write the single-line verdict to /home/user/output/validation.log (create directories if needed):

   VALIDATION PASSED            — when every element is valid  
   VALIDATION FAILED            — otherwise

   The file must contain exactly that one line followed by a newline character.

B. Availability summary  
   • Still using jq only, compute per-host uptime statistics:  
       total_checks          = count of elements for that host  
       successful_checks     = count where .status == "up"  
       success_rate_percent  = (successful_checks / total_checks)*100, rounded to 2 decimals
   • Produce /home/user/output/uptime_summary.log whose content **must follow this CSV layout exactly**:

     <host>,<total_checks>,<successful_checks>,<success_rate_percent>

     One line per distinct host, sorted in ASCII lexicographic order by <host>.  
     Example final file (spacing, commas, decimals and newline are significant):

     db-1,4,4,100.00
     web-1,4,3,75.00

   • After writing the file, also print the very same two lines to stdout so the operator can see them immediately.

Constraints & hints  
• You have standard GNU coreutils and jq; no python, node, or third-party schema tools.  
• Aim to accomplish everything with at most 3–4 shell commands (excluding harmless cd/ls/cat inspections).  
• Do not alter either original JSON file.  
• Remember to create /home/user/output if it does not exist so you can write the two log files there.

Automated checks will strictly compare:  
1) /home/user/output/validation.log (exact content)  
2) /home/user/output/uptime_summary.log (exact content and ordering)  

Nothing else will be graded, but non-conforming output files will fail the test.
truth:
Initial file system state (already present before the agent starts):

1. Directory structure
   /home/user/data
   /home/user/schemas
   (both 755, owned by user)

2. /home/user/data/ping_results.json (644, owned by user)
   Exact content:
[
  {"host":"db-1","timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:00:00Z","status":"up","latency_ms":45},
  {"host":"db-1","timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:05:00Z","status":"up","latency_ms":50},
  {"host":"db-1","timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:10:00Z","status":"up","latency_ms":47},
  {"host":"db-1","timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:15:00Z","status":"up","latency_ms":46},
  {"host":"web-1","timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:00:00Z","status":"up","latency_ms":23},
  {"host":"web-1","timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:05:00Z","status":"down","latency_ms":null},
  {"host":"web-1","timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:10:00Z","status":"up","latency_ms":25},
  {"host":"web-1","timestamp":"2023-08-01T10:15:00Z","status":"up","latency_ms":22}
]

3. /home/user/schemas/ping_result.schema.json (644, owned by user)
   Exact content:
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "array",
  "items": {
    "type": "object",
    "required": ["host", "timestamp", "status", "latency_ms"],
    "properties": {
      "host": { "type": "string" },
      "timestamp": { "type": "string" },
      "status": { "type": "string", "enum": ["up", "down"] },
      "latency_ms": { "type": ["number", "null"] }
    },
    "additionalProperties": false
  }
}

Expected deliverables after agent completes:

1. Directory /home/user/output must exist (755, user)  
2. /home/user/output/validation.log (644, user)  
   Content (with trailing newline):  
   VALIDATION PASSED\n

3. /home/user/output/uptime_summary.log (644, user)  
   Exact content (with trailing newline at the end of the second line):  
   db-1,4,4,100.00\n  
   web-1,4,3,75.00\n
